creator: edwin@mavetju.org
text:
On Wed, Sep 26, 2007 at 07:23:01PM -0400, Jason E. Hale wrote:
> I used portlint today and noticed the same string.  It brought me back to
> calculus class.  The capital letters gave it away.
> IFF is used in logic to mean "if and only if".  If I am not mistaken, that is
> the intended use here.  See: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/If_and_only_if
> Perhaps more than most porters need be familiar with.

Very understandable if you're using calculus. I'm not using calculus
here, and the rest of the line doesn't make it clear that is what
is meant.

If I don't get it, I doubt there are much others who will get it.

Edwin
